Licenses & EPR

Navigating complex waste management and industrial licensing. From Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) to hazardous waste authorizations, we ensure your facility remains compliant and fully licensed.

Get Licensed Now

What's Included

Expert management of waste licenses and industrial compliance.

PLASTIC · E-WASTE · BATTERY

EPR Compliance

Comprehensive fulfillment of Extended Producer Responsibility for Plastic, E-Waste, Battery, and Waste Oil through CPCB portal management and verified collection.

  • PIBO registration on CPCB portal
  • Fulfillment of recycling targets
  • Digital traceability of waste
  • Annual returns (Form IV) filing
  • Recycling evidence & audit support
Learn More & Case Studies →
SPCB / PCC · LICENSING

Consent to Establish / Operate (CTE / CTO)

Liaising with State Pollution Control Boards for CTE and CTO under Air & Water Acts. We ensure technical specifications meet discharge norms.

  • Effluent & Emission design audit
  • Capital investment quantification
  • SPCB online portal management
  • Technical response to SPCB queries
  • NOC procurement for expansions
Learn More & Case Studies →
RULE 9 · AUTHORISATION

Hazardous Waste Authorisation (Rule 9)

Securing and maintaining authorisations for the generation, storage, and disposal of hazardous and other wastes under Transboundary Movement Rules.

  • Form 1 & Rule 9 documentation
  • Waste characterization & mapping
  • TSDF / SLF coordination
  • Manifest & annual return filing
  • On-site waste storage audit
Learn More & Case Studies →

Circular compliance hurdles

EPR regulations are tightening globally, with heavy penalties for non-fulfillment.

01

Portal Complexity

CPCB portals for EPR registration are often technical and require precise documentation that, if flawed, can lead to immediate application rejection.

02

Credit Verification

Buying "credits" isn't enough. Ensuring those credits are from verified, authorized recyclers is critical for annual compliance audits.

03

Inventory Management

Tracking the amount of plastic or e-waste put on the market is a massive data challenge that requires a robust inventory management system.

The EPR Protocol

A technical path to total circularity.

01

Audit

  • Inventory Mapping
  • Waste Stream Audit
  • Liability Analysis
02

Registration

  • CPCB Portal Setup
  • License Filing
  • Authorized Recycler Match
03

Fulfillment

  • Credit Procurement
  • Traceability Checks
  • Annual Returns
04

Certification

  • Liability Settlement
  • Board Reporting
  • Circular Disclosure

Waste Management Rules

Technical compliance with India's evolving EPR ecosystem.

EPR Categories
  • PWM Rules 2024 Plastic Packaging (Cat I, II, III, IV)
  • E-waste Rules 2022 Electrical & Electronic Equipment (EEE)
  • Battery Waste Lead-acid, Li-ion, and Primary batteries
  • Waste Tyre Rules Producer obligations for end-of-life tyres
Operational Licensing
  • CPCB Portal Centralized PIBO registration management
  • Hazardous Waste Form 1 & Form 4 authorization
  • Biomedical Waste Authorization for healthcare and labs
  • Annual Returns Mandatory waste generation disclosures

Common Questions

Understanding your EPR and waste obligations.

Any Producer, Importer, or Brand Owner (PIBO) that uses plastic packaging for their products is obligated to register on the CPCB portal and fulfill Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) targets through recycling or reprocessing.
The CPCB levies "Environmental Compensation" (EC) based on the target shortfall. While paying EC doesn't absolve the PIBO of the EPR target (the target is carried over), it can result in significant financial impact and potential suspension of the CTO.
Hazardous waste is categorized into schedules as per the Hazardous Waste Rules 2016. Authorization is required from the SPCB to generate, store, or dispose of such waste. We help with scientific characterization and ensuring disposal is only through TSDF facilities.
CPCB Registered EPR Expert SPCB Liaison Hazardous Auth
